Regional Development

Theories, strategies, and spatial patterns of regional planning and development.

Author

Geography Team

Welcome to the Regional Development module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course examines the theories and practices of regional planning and development — addressing spatial inequalities, planning strategies, and the quest for balanced territorial development.

UGC NET Syllabus

  • Typology of Regions, Formal and Fictional Regions.
  • World Regional Disparities.
  • Theories of Regional Development (Albert O. Hirschman, Gunnar Myrdal, John Friedman), Dependency theory of Underdevelopment.
  • Global Economic Blocks.
  • Regional Development and Social Movements in India.

Growth centres and growth poles

India’s Regional Development

Planning and development in the Indian context.

Get the Presentation ↗   |   Watch the Video ↗

TipIndian Planning
  • Five Year Plans: Historical evolution of India’s approach to regional balance.
  • NITI Aayog: Successor to the Planning Commission — cooperative and competitive federalism.
  • Special Category States: North-Eastern states and hill states receiving enhanced development support.
  • Aspirational Districts Programme: Targeting India’s most underdeveloped districts for convergent action.
  • Industrial Corridors: Delhi-Mumbai (DMIC), Chennai-Bengaluru, and Amritsar-Kolkata corridors.
  • North-East Development: Look East/Act East Policy, connectivity, and border area development.

Integrated rural development programmes

Content for Integrated rural development programmes coming soon.

International Perspectives

Regional development strategies worldwide.

Get the Presentation ↗   |   Watch the Video ↗

ImportantGlobal Approaches
  • European Regional Policy: EU Structural Funds, cohesion policy, and NUTS classification system.
  • China’s Western Development Strategy: Go West policy and trade corridor investments.
  • Brazil’s Northeast Development: SUDENE and efforts to address Brazil’s historical regional divide.
  • African Development Corridors: Maputo, Lamu Port, and the SDI (Spatial Development Initiatives).

Books and Further Readings

Regional Development provides the framework for understanding and addressing spatial inequality. Continue with Settlement Geography and Indian SocioEco for related perspectives.

Regional concept in geography

Regional Concepts & Delineation

Defining and delimiting regions.

Get the Presentation ↗   |   Watch the Video ↗

NoteTypes of Regions
  • Formal (Uniform) Regions: Defined by homogeneity in one or more characteristics (climate, language, soil type).
  • Functional (Nodal) Regions: Organized around a central node — trade areas, commuting zones.
  • Planning (Administrative) Regions: Defined for governance and policy purposes.
  • Perceptual (Vernacular) Regions: Regions existing in popular culture and perception.
  • Regional Delineation Methods: Statistical, cartographic, and GIS-based approaches.

Regional development strategies

Content for Regional development strategies coming soon.

Regional Disparities

The geography of inequality.

Get the Presentation ↗   |   Watch the Video ↗

NoteMeasuring Inequality
  • Inter-Regional Disparities: Differences in income, employment, health, and education between regions.
  • Intra-Regional Disparities: Inequality within regions — urban-rural divides.
  • Indicators of Development: Per capita income, HDI, literacy, life expectancy, and poverty ratios.
  • Convergence vs. Divergence: Whether regions are growing more similar or more different over time.

Regional hierarchy

Content for Regional hierarchy coming soon.

Regional imbalances

Regional planning and development of island territories.

Content for Regional planning and development of island territories. coming soon.

Regional Planning Approaches

Strategies for addressing spatial inequality.

Get the Presentation ↗   |   Watch the Video ↗

ImportantPlanning Frameworks
  • Top-Down vs. Bottom-Up Planning: Centralized government planning vs. community-based approaches.
  • Growth Centre Strategy: Concentrating investment in selected locations to stimulate surrounding areas.
  • Integrated Area Development: Multi-sectoral, holistic development of lagging regions.
  • Backward Area Development: Special incentives and targeted programs for underdeveloped regions.
  • Participatory Planning: Stakeholder engagement and democratic decision-making in regional policy.

Theories of Regional Development

Theoretical frameworks explaining spatial inequality.

Get the Presentation ↗   |   Watch the Video ↗

TipDevelopment Models
  • Rostow’s Stages of Growth: Traditional society to age of mass consumption.
  • Myrdal’s Cumulative Causation: Backwash and spread effects reinforcing regional inequality.
  • Hirschman’s Unbalanced Growth: Polarization and trickle-down effects in development.
  • Friedmann’s Core-Periphery Model: Spatial evolution of the economy from pre-industrial to post-industrial stages.
  • Perroux’s Growth Pole Theory: Development radiating from propulsive industries in key locations.
  • Dependency Theory: Underdevelopment as a product of exploitative core-periphery relations.
